Contributing to coursepackR

Source: CONTRIBUTING.md

Thank you for your interest in improving coursepackR! This document explains how to report issues, suggest changes, and contribute code.

Reporting Bugs

If something isn’t working, please open an issue on GitHub. A helpful bug report includes:

  • The R command you ran and the exact error message
  • Output of mccourse_check() (so we can see your environment)
  • Your R version (R.version.string) and operating system
  • Steps to reproduce the problem

Suggesting Features or Improvements

Feature requests are welcome! Open a GitHub issue describing:

  • What you’d like the package to do
  • Why it would be useful for students or instructors
  • Any ideas for how it might work

Contributing Code

Development Setup

  1. Fork the repository on GitHub.

  2. Clone your fork locally:

    git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/coursepackR.git
cd coursepackR

  3. Install development dependencies:

    install.packages(c("devtools", "roxygen2", "testthat", "pkgdown"))

  4. Load the package for interactive development:

    devtools::load_all()

Code Style

  • Documentation: Use roxygen2 for all exported functions. Include @param, @return, @examples, and @export tags.
  • User-facing messages: Use the cli package (cli::cli_alert_success(), cli::cli_alert_danger(), etc.) — not message(), cat(), or print().
  • Errors and warnings: Use rlang::abort() and rlang::warn() instead of stop() and warning().
  • Tests: Use testthat (edition 3). Put tests in tests/testthat/test-*.R.
  • File paths: Always use file.path() — never paste paths with / or \\.

Making Changes

  1. Create a branch for your changes:

    git checkout -b fix/describe-your-change

  2. Make your edits in R/, man/, vignettes/, or tests/.

  3. Rebuild documentation if you changed any roxygen2 comments:

    devtools::document()

  4. Run tests:

    devtools::test()

  5. Run R CMD check:

    devtools::check()

    Aim for 0 errors, 0 warnings, and 0 notes.

  6. Commit and push:

    git add -A
git commit -m "Brief description of change"
git push origin fix/describe-your-change

  7. Open a pull request on GitHub against the main branch. Describe what you changed and why.

Pull Request Checklist

Questions?

Open an issue or contact the maintainer at aleith@siue.edu.
